commitMessage: Revert "Reapply "Reapply "Add R key to reset game from any state"""

topics.title: Remove reset keyboard shortcut and revert related UI changes
topics.trigger: A previously added feature allowing players to reset the snake game at any time by pressing the R key is being rolled back, along with its associated on-screen instructions.
topics.response:
- Removed the R key handler from the keyboard input logic in `src/main.ts`, which had triggered a full game reset from any state (playing, paused, or game over).
- Removed a line in `src/main.ts` that hid the pause screen overlay when starting a new game, which was added as part of the same feature.
- Updated the controls hint text in `index.html` to remove the "Press R to Reset" instruction.
topics.decisions: The decision to revert rather than refine suggests the reset-from-any-state behavior introduced an unacceptable side effect or regression — likely the pause screen not being properly dismissed, or the R key conflicting with expected game state transitions. Rolling back the entire feature (including the UI hint) keeps the game's state management clean and avoids shipping a partially broken experience.

recap: The ability to reset the snake game at any time using the R key was removed. This keyboard shortcut had been added and reapplied across multiple commits, but this change rolls it back entirely — the on-screen control hint no longer mentions a reset key, and pressing R during a game has no effect. A related fix to how the pause overlay was dismissed when starting a new game was also undone as part of the rollback.
